extra.reason: All new MlbStatsConnector methods implemented inline with the base copy in connectors.py
Superseded by pitcher-streamer-e60 — fixing start_count is a direct consequence of implementing confirmed probable pitcher fetching; no separate fix needed

pymlb-statsapi generates method signatures from the MLB Stats API Swagger schema, which does not include 'hydrate' as a named param. The library silently drops any kwargs not in the schema, making it impossible to use for our fetch_schedule_with_venue call that depends on hydrate='venue(location),weather,probablePitchers'. Migrating would be a regression. Our current httpx.get approach is simpler and supports all the params we need. The probable-pitcher features (e60, 2t1, etc.) can proceed without this library — they'll use the same httpx pattern.

Fixed _normalize_player and _normalize_roster to use eligible_positions (list) instead of display_position (doesn't exist in Yahoo's API), and name as plain string instead of dict. Added INFO logging for FA raw count, team_id filter result, and DEBUG logging for mlbam_id misses and starter gate exclusions. Tests updated to match Yahoo's actual data format.

Implemented 4-phase parallel pipeline using ThreadPoolExecutor: Phase 1 parallelizes schedule+team_IDs+roster+FA (was 4 sequential calls), Phase 2 parallelizes mlbam_id resolution for all candidates, Phase 3 parallelizes game log fetches for all mlbam_ids, Phase 4 parallelizes batting splits+weather for all unique (opponent, handedness) and venue pairs. Removed now-unused _build_team_maps helper. All 68 tests pass.

Implemented probable-pitcher filtering in _extract_pitcher_starts: games where another pitcher is the confirmed probable are now excluded for this pitcher. This fixes start_count=6-7 (was returning all team games). is_probable=True marks confirmed starters; games with no probable set are kept as open slots. The existing schedule hydration already provided probable_home_id/probable_away_id from fetch_schedule_with_venue — no pymlb-statsapi needed. Added regression test verifying cross-pitcher exclusion. 69 tests pass.

Implemented rotation.py with build_team_rotation() and project_probable_pitchers(). build_team_rotation groups confirmed starters by team, sorts by last_start desc, excludes pitchers >8 days stale, assigns slot numbers. project_probable_pitchers greedily assigns eligible pitchers to open game slots, respecting next_eligible date and confirmed_probable_ids exclusion. Wired into main.py Phase 5 — projected starts flow through _extract_pitcher_starts (now accepts projected_home/projected_away dicts) with is_projected=True flag. Template shows ~ badge for projected starts. 79 tests pass.

Implemented off-day shifting in project_probable_pitchers via _compute_next_start_with_offdays: counts off-days in the 5-day window and extends projection accordingly. Now uses best-match logic (closest projected date <= game_date) instead of greedy index cycling. Each game_pk is treated independently (covers doubleheaders). Tests added for off-day shifting, confirmation exclusion, and ineligible pitcher cases. 80 tests pass.

Added fetch_recent_transactions() to MlbStatsConnector: fetches trailing 7 days of MLB transactions, returns set of mlbam_ids marked IL/DFA/optioned. Passed as unavailable_ids to project_probable_pitchers(), which excludes those pitchers from open slot assignment. Fetched in Phase 1 parallel block (adds ~0ms latency since it runs alongside schedule/teams). 2 unit tests added.

Added confidence field to every annotated start dict in _score_pitcher_starts: CONFIRMED (is_probable=True), PROJECTED (is_projected=True), UNKNOWN (neither). The field is populated from the is_probable/is_projected flags set by _extract_pitcher_starts, which in turn reads from probable_home/away (MLB API) and projected_home/away (rotation math).

Updated index.html: replaced .probable/.start-cell CSS and ★ probable-star with three-tier system. .confirmed=bold+orange★, .projected=italic+purple~, .unknown=grey+? badge with dimmed score. Uses s.confidence | default('UNKNOWN') Jinja2 filter. Old ★ still rendered for CONFIRMED starts so test_probable_star_rendered continues to pass. 82 tests pass.

Extracted all data fetching into _build_pitcher_data(park_factors) function. Background daemon thread started in lifespan: calls _build_pitcher_data every 2 hours, stores result in app.state.pitcher_cache. Route index() reads from cache (instant response); on cold start (cache=None), fetches synchronously then caches. Template shows 'Updated HH:MM AM/PM' from fetched_at timestamp. 82 tests pass — background thread is daemon so exits with test process, mocks are active during thread's first refresh.
